page: Hetzner Cloud ccx33 is a dedicated CPU server instance built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors. It delivers 8 dedicated vCPUs, mapped from 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance is provisioned with 31.0 GB of RAM, translating to 3.91 GB of memory per core, and includes 240 GB of local SSD storage. Virtualization is managed via the QEMU hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 3061.27 and a strong bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 13.79 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is average, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 12273.64 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 7266.0. Memory bandwidth tests show an average read-only small block cached speed of 585.69 GB/sec, but a weak uncached large block read speed of 38.5 GB/sec. Database operations and web serving throughput are average, with Redis SET operations reaching 11.09 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 11.3 GB/sec.

The ccx33 instance offers dedicated CPU resources, ensuring consistent performance without noisy-neighbor interference, which represents a highly stable resource density for general-purpose workloads. However, a notable tradeoff is the weak uncached large-block memory read bandwidth and weak prompt processing speeds for medium-sized 7B LLM models. This server is suitable for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, text processing, database operations, and moderate web serving, but is less optimal for memory-intensive large-block data processing or larger machine learning inference tasks.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx33 is a dedicated CPU server instance utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan architecture. It is configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores, 2 threads per core) running at 2.0 GHz, 31.0 GB of RAM, and 240 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, alongside average multi-core and database execution speeds. However, uncached large-block memory read bandwidth and medium LLM prompt processing are relatively weak. With dedicated CPU allocation and a balanced memory-to-core ratio, this instance provides stable resource density for workloads such as software compilation, text processing, database operations, and static web serving, while avoiding the performance fluctuations of shared-core environments.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx33 is a dedicated CPU server featuring 8 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 31.0 GB of RAM, and 240 GB of local SSD storage, delivering stable performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx33 is a dedicated CPU server featuring 8 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 31.0 GB of RAM, and 240 GB of local SSD storage for stable workloads.
tagline: Dedicated AMD EPYC processor cores and balanced memory capacity for stable, predictable general-purpose computing and database workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
31.0 GB of system memory and 240 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Average multi-core execution, database operations, and web serving throughput
Tradeoff of weak uncached large-block memory read bandwidth
Suitable for software compilation, text processing, and database hosting
